mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current payroll level at 132989 thousand persons indicates sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current retail sales at 408039 million dollars
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS signals produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% year-over-year, the Fed is tightening into weak inflation, signaling policy error risk and equity market uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and CPIAUCSL at 229.177 as of October 2017
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with subdued inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following trigger activation are positive in > 60% of instances, or the interaction effect (joint condition) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 2% year-over-year indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with supply acceleration
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 2% do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than months with growth < 2% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1.0 million annualized units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 654.0 thousand units as of September 2017
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing demand deterioration
failure_condition: Periods with HOUST < 1.0 million produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month AND PAYEMS growth < 100 thousand month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% and PAYEMS at 132989 thousand as of October 2017
failure_condition: Joint trigger activation produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression predicting return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 1.0% combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint trigger are positive in > 65% of instances, or multivariate analysis shows the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13502.4 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2017
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than quarters with growth < 2.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 24-month window.
